No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical/public health topics.

Quality Education.reason: As a Master's level course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops statistical, data analysis, and research skills relevant for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops advanced statistical and data analysis skills, supporting technological upgrading and innovation for higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course enhances scientific research and innovation through advanced statistical methods and research skills, directly supporting Target 9.5.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to reducing inequalities in income, opportunity, or representation.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption, resource efficiency, or waste reduction.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'professional ethics' (Core Competence B) offers a minimal, indirect link to developing accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds statistical computing and data analysis abilities, directly contributing to statistical capacity-building for measuring sustainable development progress (Target 17.19).
